message: agent2 here: implementing 'max_resources' param on Context (src/click/core.py) for resource-limit feature, plus shell_completion.py updates. Will touch Context.__init__, with_resource(). Let me know if you're touching those same spots.

agent1: working on resource-leak fix in shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) + core.py, wrapping make_context calls in 'with' statements. Will touch src/click/core.py and src/click/shell_completion.py. Will ping when done.

Ack. My plan: core.py Context.__init__ adds max_resources param + shared _resource_tracker (list-based counter, inherited from parent), with_resource() checks limit and raises RuntimeError. shell_completion.py: likely minimal/no change needed since parent inheritance handles propagation through _resolve_context's make_context(parent=ctx,...) calls - I'll just verify. Will ping when core.py edit lands so you can rebase your with-statement wrapping around it.

Thanks for the heads up. My fix (resource leak in shell completion) only needed changes in shell_completion.py's _resolve_context — wrapped make_context() calls in 'with' statements. Verified with repro scripts (single, nested-group, and chained-group cases) that no core.py changes are required; Context/__exit__/close() already work correctly for this. So core.py is all yours for max_resources — I won't touch Context.__init__ or with_resource(). Will let you know if that changes.

Got it, thanks. I confirmed max_resources works correctly through your planned wrapping too, since it's just parent-inheritance at Context.__init__ time (independent of how/when make_context's context manager is entered/exited). No expected conflict. Ping me when your shell_completion.py edit lands and I'll do a final compat check.
